docs.title: swclib
docs.text:
swclib is a Python library for processing, converting, and evaluating neuron morphology data in SWC format.

SWC is the standard file format in computational neuroscience for representing 3D reconstructions of neuronal structures. Each neuron is described as a tree of labeled 3D points, encoding the soma, axons, and dendrites along with their spatial coordinates and radii.

docs.title: What swclib provides
docs.text: Module Description swclib.data Load, manipulate, and traverse SWC trees and forests swclib.image Bidirectional conversion between SWC and 3D binary mask images swclib.metrics Quantitative comparison of neuron reconstructions swclib.whole_brain Parallel reader for large tiled TIFF image stacks

docs.title: Typical workflow
docs.text:
Raw Image Volume
      │
      ▼
  Segmentation / Reconstruction Algorithm
      │
      ▼
   SWC File  ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
      │                                                     │
      ▼                                                     ▼
swclib.image                                         swclib.metrics
(SWC ↔ Mask)                                    (compare with gold standard)
      │                                                     │
      ▼                                                     ▼
 3D Mask (.tif)                                    Precision / Recall / F1

docs.title: SWC format overview
docs.text:
Each line in an SWC file encodes one node:

id   type   x   y   z   radius   parent_id

Standard node types:

Type Meaning 1 Soma 2 Axon 3 Basal dendrite 4 Apical dendrite 5 Undefined / other

Root nodes use parent_id = -1. A single SWC file may contain one tree (single soma) or multiple disconnected trees (forest).

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: See Installation and Quick Start.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python >= 3.8
  • pip or conda

docs.title: Install via pip
docs.text: pip install swclib

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/yang-ze-kang/swclib.git
cd swclib
pip install -e .

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
swclib requires the following packages, which are installed automatically:

Package Purpose numpy Array operations scipy Spatial indexing (KDTree), signal processing networkx Graph-based skeletonization anytree Tree data structure miniball Minimum bounding sphere rtree Spatial index for edge matching rasterio Tiled TIFF I/O tifffile TIFF image reading/writing scikit-image Image skeletonization and filtering opencv-python Image rasterization tqdm Progress bars

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text:
import swclib
print("swclib installed successfully")

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This page walks through the most common use cases in a few lines of code.

docs.title: Load and inspect an SWC file
docs.text:
from swclib.data.swc import Swc

swc = Swc("neuron.swc")

print(f"Nodes:        {len(swc.nodes)}")
print(f"Total length: {swc.length:.2f} µm")
print(f"Bounding box: {swc.bound_box}")

docs.title: Scale to physical units
docs.text:
SWC files often store coordinates in voxel indices. Use rescale to convert to physical units (e.g., micrometers):

# voxel size: 0.5 µm in XY, 0.35 µm in Z
swc.rescale((0.5, 0.5, 0.35))

docs.title: Resample to uniform node spacing
docs.text:
swc.resample(min_distance=2.0)   # one node every 2 µm
swc.save_to_swc("neuron_resampled.swc")

docs.title: Convert SWC to 3D binary mask
docs.text:
from swclib.image.swc2mask import Swc2Mask

converter = Swc2Mask(shape=(300, 300, 300), scale=(1, 1, 1), radius=1)
mask = converter.run("neuron.swc", out_file="neuron_mask.tif")

docs.title: Convert 3D mask back to SWC
docs.text:
from swclib.image.mask2swc import Mask2Swc
import tifffile

mask = tifffile.imread("neuron_mask.tif")
converter = Mask2Swc(voxel_size=(1.0, 1.0, 1.0))
converter.run(mask, "neuron_reconstructed.swc")

docs.title: Compare two reconstructions
docs.text:
from swclib.metrics.fiber_metric import FiberMetric

metric = FiberMetric(iou_threshold=0.8, dist_threshold=5.0)
result = metric.run("gold.swc", "predicted.swc")

print(f"Precision: {result['precision']:.3f}")
print(f"Recall:    {result['recall']:.3f}")
print(f"F1 score:  {result['f1_score']:.3f}")

docs.title: Batch evaluation
docs.text:
from swclib.metrics.manager import MetricManager

manager = MetricManager(
    metric_names=["ssd", "length", "keypoints", "fiber"],
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
)

pairs = [("gold1.swc", "pred1.swc"), ("gold2.swc", "pred2.swc")]
for gold, pred in pairs:
    manager.add_data(gold, pred)

summary = manager.collect(save_path="results.json")

Continue reading the Tutorial for detailed explanations.

docs.title: swclib.data.swc.Swc
docs.text: Lightweight dict-based representation of an SWC file.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
Swc(file_name=None)
Parameter Type Description file_name str or None Path to SWC file. If None, creates empty instance.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description nodes dict[int, dict] Node dict keyed by node ID edges list[tuple] List of (node_id, parent_id) bound_box list[float] [min_x, min_y, min_z, max_x, max_y, max_z] file_name str Source file path length float Property. Total path length.

docs.title: open(file_name)
docs.text:
Parse and load an SWC text file.

Parameter Type Description file_name str Path to SWC file

docs.title: rescale(scale)
docs.text:
Scale all node coordinates by (sx, sy, sz).

Parameter Type Description scale tuple[float, float, float] Scale factors (sx, sy, sz)

docs.title: add_offset(offset)
docs.text:
Translate all node coordinates.

Parameter Type Description offset tuple[float, float, float] Translation (ox, oy, oz)

docs.title: resample(min_distance=2.0, in_place=True)
docs.text:
Upsample or downsample the tree so consecutive nodes are approximately min_distance apart.

Parameter Type Default Description min_distance float 2.0 Target spacing in_place bool True Modify in place or return new Swc

docs.title: remove_duplicate_nodes(use_radius=False, round_ndigits=None, reindex=False, in_place=True)
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description use_radius bool False Include radius in duplicate check round_ndigits int or None None Round coordinates before comparison reindex bool False Reassign node IDs after removal in_place bool True Modify in place or return new Swc

docs.title: get_coords() → ndarray
docs.text: Returns all node coordinates as an array of shape (N, 3).

docs.title: get_roots(return_coords=True)
docs.text:
Returns nodes with parent_id == -1.

Parameter Type Default Description return_coords bool True If True, returns ndarray; else returns list of node dicts

docs.title: get_density(dis=10.0, p=0.3, exclude_hops=1) → float
docs.text: Estimates local spatial density of the neuron.

docs.title: check_min_distance_between_non_adjacent_nodes(threshold=5.0) → tuple
docs.text: Returns (min_distance, pairs) where pairs is a list of node ID pairs closer than threshold.

docs.title: save_to_swc(out_path, sort_by_id=True, write_header=True, float_fmt='.6f', mkdir=True, reindex=False, radius=None) → str
docs.text: Write the SWC to disk. Returns the output path.

docs.title: merge_swcs(swcs, offsets=None, keep_file_name=True) → Swc
docs.text: Parameter Type Description swcs list[Swc] List of Swc objects to merge offsets list[tuple] or None Per-file translation offsets keep_file_name bool Preserve file names in merged result

docs.title: swclib.data.swc_forest.SwcForest
docs.text: Object-oriented tree representation. Handles both single-root and multi-root (disconnected) SWC files.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
SwcForest(swc=None)
Parameter Type Description swc str, Swc, or None File path, Swc object, or empty

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description roots list[SwcNode] List of root nodes id_set set[int] All node IDs scale tuple Applied scaling

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Returns Description size() int Number of nodes length(force_update=False) float Total path length get_edge_num() int Number of edges contains(nid) bool Node ID membership test get_node_list(update=False) list[SwcNode] All nodes (cached) get_node_by_nid(nid) SwcNode ID → node lookup get_branch_nodes() list[SwcNode] Nodes with ≥ 2 children get_leaf_nodes() list[SwcNode] Nodes with 0 children get_roots(return_coords=True) list[SwcNode] or ndarray Root nodes get_somas() list[SwcSoma] Soma regions get_fibers(only_from_soma=False, min_length=0.0) list[SwcFiber] All root-to-leaf paths get_fibers_by_roi(roi) list[SwcFiber] Fibers passing through ROI get_fiber_by_leaf(leaf, roi=None) SwcFiber Path to a specific leaf get_nearest_node(coord, subtree_root=None, topk=1) SwcNode or list KD-tree nearest neighbor get_copy() SwcForest Deep copy rescale(scale) — Scale coordinates relocation(offset) — Translate coordinates sort_node_list(key='default') — Reorder/reindex nodes get_lca_preprocess(node_num) — Initialize LCA data structure get_lca(u, v) int Lowest Common Ancestor of nodes u and v save_to_file(path) — Export to SWC file

docs.title: swclib.data.swc_node.SwcNode
docs.text: A single node in the tree.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description nid int Node ID ntype int Node type (1–5) coord EuclideanPoint3D 3D coordinates radius float Node radius parent SwcNode or None Parent node root_length float Accumulated path length from root

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Returns Description is_root() bool True if node has no valid parent is_regular() bool True if nid >= 0 distance(target, mode='3D') float Distance to another node parent_distance() float Distance to parent get_subtree_node_list() list[SwcNode] All descendants get_subtree_length(force_update=False) float Subtree path length get_subtree_leafs(roi=None) list[SwcNode] Terminal nodes get_subtree_fibers(roi=None, with_root=False) list[SwcFiber] Paths to all leaves get_fiber_by_leaf(roi=None) SwcFiber Path from root to this leaf get_rerooted_tree(nid_start=1) SwcNode Reroot tree at this node to_swc_str(pid=None, scale=(1,1,1)) str Format as SWC line

docs.title: Module-level function
docs.text: nodes2coords(nodes) → ndarray  # shape (N, 3)

docs.title: swclib.data.swc_fiber.SwcFiber
docs.text: A root-to-leaf path.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description nodes list[SwcNode] Ordered nodes from root to leaf length float Property. Total path length coords ndarray Property. Coordinates, shape (N, 3) center ndarray Property. Mean coordinate

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Returns Description append(node) — Add node to end pop() — Remove last node reverse() — Reverse node order copy() SwcFiber Shallow copy get_nearest_node(point, return_dist=False) SwcNode or (SwcNode, float) Nearest node on fiber cal_iou(fiber, dist_sample=1.0, dist_threshold=3.0) float IoU with another fiber get_overlap_length_with(fiber, dist_sample=1.0, dist_threshold=3.0) float Overlap length is_sub_fiber_of(fiber, ...) bool or float Whether this fiber is a sub-path to_str_list(scale=(1,1,1)) str Format as SWC lines

save_somas_to_file(somas, path, scale=(1,1,1))

docs.title: swclib.image.swc2mask.Swc2Mask
docs.text: Converts an SWC neuron to a 3D binary mask volume.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
Swc2Mask(shape=(300, 300, 300), scale=(1, 1, 1), radius=1, method='line')
Parameter Type Default Description shape tuple[int, int, int] (300,300,300) Output volume shape (Z, Y, X) scale tuple[float, float, float] (1,1,1) Voxel size (sx, sy, sz) radius float 1 Neurite rendering radius method str 'line' Rendering method: 'line' or 'sphere_cone'

docs.title: run(swc, out_file=None) → ndarray
docs.text:
Parameter Type Description swc str or Swc SWC file path or object out_file str or None If given, saves result as TIFF

Returns: ndarray of shape (Z, Y, X), dtype uint8.

docs.title: swc_to_mask_line(swc, shape, scale, max_radius) → ndarray
docs.text:
Distance-transform–based line rendering.

Parameter Type Description swc Swc Source SWC object shape tuple Output shape (Z, Y, X) scale tuple Voxel size max_radius float Cutoff radius

docs.title: swc_to_mask_sphere_cone(swc, shape, foreground_value, r_scale) → ndarray
docs.text:
Sphere + truncated cone rendering.

Parameter Type Description swc Swc Source SWC object shape tuple Output shape (Z, Y, X) foreground_value int Voxel value for foreground r_scale float Radius scale factor

docs.title: swclib.image.mask2swc.Mask2Swc
docs.text: Converts a 3D binary mask to an SWC reconstruction.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
Mask2Swc(
    voxel_size=(1.0, 1.0, 1.0),
    connectivity=26,
    thres_fiber_min_len=20.0,
    thres_branch_min_len=15.0,
    thres_segment_merge_angle=None,
    thres_segment_merge_dist=None,
    smooth_window_size=None,
    smooth_poly=None,
    node_sample_distance=2.0,
)
Parameter Type Default Description voxel_size tuple (1,1,1) Physical voxel size (sx, sy, sz) in µm connectivity int 26 Neighbor connectivity for skeleton: 6 or 26 thres_fiber_min_len float 20.0 Min length (µm) to keep an isolated fiber thres_branch_min_len float 15.0 Min length (µm) to keep a side branch thres_segment_merge_angle float or None None Max angle (°) for fiber merging thres_segment_merge_dist float or None None Max endpoint distance for merging smooth_window_size int or None None Savitzky-Golay smoothing window smooth_poly int or None None Savitzky-Golay polynomial order node_sample_distance float 2.0 Output node spacing after resampling

docs.title: run(mask, swc_path, soma_path=None, radius=0.1, verbose=False) → str
docs.text:
Runs the full conversion pipeline and writes the SWC file.

Parameter Type Description mask ndarray 3D binary mask, shape (Z, Y, X) swc_path str Output SWC file path soma_path str or None Optional soma annotation file radius float Default node radius in output SWC verbose bool Print pipeline statistics

Returns: Output SWC file path (str).

docs.title: build_graph_from_skeleton(skel, connectivity, voxel_size) → nx.Graph
docs.text: Builds a NetworkX graph from a skeletonized binary mask.

docs.title: remove_isolated_nodes(G) → (nx.Graph, list)
docs.text: Removes degree-0 nodes.

docs.title: merge_fibers(G, voxel_size, ...) → (nx.Graph, int)
docs.text: Merges nearby near-collinear fiber endpoints. Returns (graph, num_merged).

docs.title: remove_short_fibers(G, voxel_size, thres_min_len) → (nx.Graph, int)
docs.text: Removes fibers shorter than the threshold.

docs.title: remove_short_branchs(G, voxel_size, thres_min_len) → (nx.Graph, int)
docs.text: Prunes side branches shorter than the threshold.

docs.title: refine_fibers(G, smooth_window_size, smooth_poly, node_sample_distance, voxel_size) → nx.Graph
docs.text: Applies Savitzky-Golay smoothing and uniform resampling to all fibers.

docs.title: API Reference — Metrics
docs.text:
All metrics share the same base interface:

result = metric.run(gold_swc_path, pred_swc_path)

Returns a dict.

docs.title: swclib.metrics.ssd_metric.SSDMetric
docs.text: Spatial distance metric.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
SSDMetric(min_distance=2.0, scale=(1, 1, 1))
Parameter Type Default Description min_distance float 2.0 Resampling step and SSD threshold (µm) scale tuple (1,1,1) Coordinate scaling (sx, sy, sz)

docs.title: run(gold, pred) → dict
docs.text:
Parameter Type Description gold str Gold standard SWC path pred str Predicted SWC path

Returns:

Key Type Description sd float Mean bidirectional spatial distance sd_gt2pred float Mean distance gold → pred sd_pred2gt float Mean distance pred → gold ssd float Mean of distances exceeding min_distance ssd_gt2pred float SSD in gold→pred direction ssd_pred2gt float SSD in pred→gold direction ssd_percent float Fraction of nodes with distance > min_distance

docs.title: swclib.metrics.length_metric.LengthMetric
docs.text: Edge-level path coverage metric.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
LengthMetric(
    radius_threshold=5.0,
    length_threshold=2.0,
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
    resample_step=1.0,
)
Parameter Type Default Description radius_threshold float 5.0 Spatial tolerance for edge matching (µm) length_threshold float 2.0 Minimum edge length to evaluate (µm) scale tuple (1,1,1) Coordinate scaling resample_step float 1.0 Resampling step for edge comparison

docs.title: run(gold, pred) → dict
docs.text:
Returns:

Key Type Description precision float Predicted length that matches gold recall float Gold length covered by prediction f1_score float Harmonic mean of precision and recall TP int Matched edge count FP int Unmatched predicted edge count FN int Unmatched gold edge count num_gt int Total gold edges num_pred int Total predicted edges

docs.title: swclib.metrics.keypoint_metric.KeypointMetric
docs.text: Branch and leaf node detection metric.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
KeypointMetric(
    keypoint_types=["branch", "leaf"],
    threshold_dis=5.0,
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
    use_category=False,
)
Parameter Type Default Description keypoint_types list[str] ["branch", "leaf"] Which keypoints to evaluate threshold_dis float 5.0 Matching distance threshold (µm) scale tuple (1,1,1) Coordinate scaling use_category bool False Report per-type stats separately

docs.title: run(gold, pred) → dict
docs.text:
Combined mode (use_category=False):

Key Type Description precision float Detection precision recall float Detection recall f1 float F1 score TP / FP / FN int Matched / extra / missed keypoints num_gt int Total gold keypoints num_pred int Total predicted keypoints

Per-category mode (use_category=True):

Result contains nested dicts result["branch"] and result["leaf"], each with the same keys above.

docs.title: swclib.metrics.fiber_metric.FiberMetric
docs.text: Fiber-level (root-to-leaf path) matching metric.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
FiberMetric(
    iou_threshold=0.8,
    dist_threshold=5.0,
    dist_sample=1.0,
    align_roots=True,
    align_roots_threshold=20.0,
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
    resample_step=2.0,
    only_from_soma=False,
    with_direction=False,
    use_category=False,
    min_fiber_length=0.0,
)
Parameter Type Default Description iou_threshold float 0.8 Minimum IoU for a match to count as TP dist_threshold float 5.0 Spatial tolerance for IoU calculation (µm) dist_sample float 1.0 Resampling step for IoU calculation align_roots bool True Align tree roots before comparison align_roots_threshold float 20.0 Max distance to match roots scale tuple (1,1,1) Coordinate scaling resample_step float 2.0 Tree resampling step only_from_soma bool False Only evaluate fibers from type=1 nodes with_direction bool False Consider fiber direction in matching use_category bool False Report axon / dendrite stats separately min_fiber_length float 0.0 Ignore fibers shorter than this (µm)

docs.title: run(gold, pred, skip_center_dist=100, return_fibers=False, verbose=False) → dict
docs.text:
Parameter Type Description gold str Gold standard SWC path pred str Predicted SWC path skip_center_dist float Skip pairs whose soma centers are farther than this return_fibers bool Include SwcFiber objects in result verbose bool Print matching progress

Returns:

Key Type Description precision float Fiber precision recall float Fiber recall f1_score float Harmonic mean TP / FP / FN int Matched / extra / missed fibers num_gt int Total gold fibers num_pred int Total predicted fibers iou_matched float Mean IoU of matched pairs iou_all float Mean IoU across all gold fibers ious list[float] Per-gold-fiber IoU values matches list Matched (gold_fiber, pred_fiber) pairs FN_fiber_ids list[list[int]] Node IDs of unmatched gold fibers gold_fibers list[SwcFiber] or None Returned when return_fibers=True pred_fibers list[SwcFiber] or None Returned when return_fibers=True

With use_category=True, nested dicts result["axon"] and result["dendrite"] contain per-type stats.

docs.title: swclib.metrics.manager.MetricManager
docs.text: Batch evaluation manager.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
MetricManager(
    metric_names=["ssd", "length", "keypoints", "fiber"],
    collect_method="micro",
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
)
Parameter Type Default Description metric_names list[str] All four Metrics to compute collect_method str "micro" "micro" (aggregate TP/FP/FN) or "macro" (average P/R/F1) scale tuple (1,1,1) Coordinate scaling applied to all metrics

docs.title: update_metric_params(metric_params)
docs.text:
Override default parameters for individual metrics.

manager.update_metric_params({
    "fiber": {"iou_threshold": 0.9, "dist_threshold": 3.0},
    "keypoints": {"threshold_dis": 3.0},
})

docs.title: add_data(swc_gt, swc_pred)
docs.text: Compute metrics for one gold/pred pair and store results.

docs.title: collect(save_path=None) → dict
docs.text: Aggregate stored results into a summary dict. Optionally save to JSON.

docs.title: swclib.whole_brain.tifreader.WBTReader
docs.text: Parallel reader for large 3D image stacks stored as 2D TIFF slices.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
WBTReader(slice_dir, slice_name_pattern)
Parameter Type Description slice_dir str Directory containing 2D TIFF slice files slice_name_pattern str Regex with one capture group matching the Z index

Example pattern: r"slice_(\d+)\.tif" matches slice_0042.tif with Z index 42.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description slice_dir str Slice directory slice_name_pattern str Regex pattern start_z int Minimum Z index found end_z int Maximum Z index found height int Slice height (Y dimension) width int Slice width (X dimension) depth int Number of slices (Z dimension) dtype dtype NumPy dtype of slice data

docs.title: get_dimensions() → tuple
docs.text: Returns (depth, height, width).

docs.title: get_bbox() → tuple
docs.text: Returns ((x1, y1, z1), (x2, y2, z2)) bounding box.

docs.title: read_region(start, end, mode='tiff', num_workers=32, parallel_backend='thread', padding=None) → ndarray
docs.text:
Read a 3D sub-region from the slice stack.

Parameter Type Default Description start tuple[int, int, int] — Inclusive start corner (x, y, z) end tuple[int, int, int] — Exclusive end corner (x, y, z) mode str 'tiff' Backend: 'tiff' (tifffile) or 'raster' (rasterio) num_workers int 32 Number of parallel workers parallel_backend str 'thread' 'thread' or 'process' padding tuple or None None Optional padding (px, py, pz) added to each side

Returns: ndarray of shape (z2-z1, y2-y1, x2-x1).

docs.title: swclib.whole_brain.swc_reader.SwcReader
docs.text: Minimal SWC reader for large whole-brain coordinate files.

docs.title: SwcReader.read(path) → ndarray
docs.text:
Parameter Type Description path str SWC file path

Returns: ndarray of shape (N, 7) with columns [id, type, x, y, z, radius, parent].

docs.title: Loading and Manipulating SWC Data
docs.text:
swclib provides two levels of data representation, each suited for different use cases:

Class Location Best for Swc swclib.data.swc File I/O, coordinate transforms, simple queries SwcForest swclib.data.swc_forest Tree algorithms, fiber extraction, LCA queries

docs.title: Swc — Lightweight dict-based representation
docs.text: Swc stores nodes and edges as plain dicts. It is the fastest way to read, transform, and write SWC files.

docs.title: Loading
docs.text:
from swclib.data.swc import Swc

swc = Swc("path/to/neuron.swc")

Each node is accessible via swc.nodes[node_id] as a dict with keys: id, type, x, y, z, radius, parent.

docs.title: Coordinate transforms
docs.text:
# Scale to physical units (voxel size in µm)
swc.rescale((0.5, 0.5, 0.35))

# Translate by offset
swc.add_offset((100.0, 200.0, 50.0))

docs.title: Resampling
docs.text:
resample inserts or removes nodes so that consecutive nodes are approximately min_distance apart.

swc.resample(min_distance=2.0)

docs.title: Removing duplicate nodes
docs.text:
# Duplicates based on XYZ coordinates only
swc.remove_duplicate_nodes()

# Also consider radius when detecting duplicates
swc.remove_duplicate_nodes(use_radius=True)

# Round coordinates before comparison
swc.remove_duplicate_nodes(round_ndigits=2)

docs.title: Querying nodes and structure
docs.text:
# All node coordinates as (N, 3) array
coords = swc.get_coords()

# Root nodes (parent_id == -1)
roots = swc.get_roots(return_coords=True)   # returns ndarray
roots = swc.get_roots(return_coords=False)  # returns list of node dicts

# Total path length
print(swc.length)

# Bounding box: [min_x, min_y, min_z, max_x, max_y, max_z]
print(swc.bound_box)

docs.title: Spatial density
docs.text:
# Fraction of volume within 'dis' µm that contains neuron nodes
density = swc.get_density(dis=10.0, p=0.3, exclude_hops=1)

docs.title: Detecting crossings
docs.text:
# Find pairs of non-adjacent nodes that are closer than 'threshold'
min_dist, pairs = swc.check_min_distance_between_non_adjacent_nodes(threshold=5.0)

docs.title: Merging multiple SWC files
docs.text:
from swclib.data.swc import merge_swcs

swc1 = Swc("cell1.swc")
swc2 = Swc("cell2.swc")

# Merge without offset
merged = merge_swcs([swc1, swc2])

# Merge with per-file translations
merged = merge_swcs([swc1, swc2], offsets=[(0, 0, 0), (500, 0, 0)])
merged.save_to_swc("merged.swc")

docs.title: Saving
docs.text:
swc.save_to_swc(
    "output.swc",
    sort_by_id=True,
    write_header=True,
    float_fmt=".6f",
    mkdir=True,
    reindex=False,
)

docs.title: SwcForest — Object-oriented tree representation
docs.text: SwcForest wraps SWC data in a proper tree structure using anytree. It handles both single-root and multi-root (disconnected) SWC files, making it the standard representation for topology-aware operations.

docs.title: Loading
docs.text:
from swclib.data.swc_forest import SwcForest

# From file path
forest = SwcForest("neuron.swc")

# From an existing Swc object
forest = SwcForest(swc)

docs.title: Basic properties
docs.text:
print(forest.size())           # number of nodes
print(forest.length())         # total path length
print(forest.get_edge_num())   # number of edges
print(len(forest.roots))       # number of root nodes

docs.title: Accessing nodes
docs.text:
# All nodes (cached list)
nodes = forest.get_node_list()

# Dict lookup by node ID
node = forest.get_node_by_nid(42)

# Branch nodes (degree > 1) and leaf nodes (degree 0)
branches = forest.get_branch_nodes()
leaves   = forest.get_leaf_nodes()

docs.title: Nearest-neighbor queries
docs.text:
# Nearest node to a 3D point
nearest = forest.get_nearest_node([x, y, z])

# Top-k nearest
results = forest.get_nearest_node([x, y, z], topk=5)
# returns list of (SwcNode, distance)

docs.title: Soma detection
docs.text:
Nodes with type == 1 define the soma. get_somas extracts connected soma regions:

somas = forest.get_somas()
for soma in somas:
    print(soma.center.coord)

docs.title: Fiber extraction
docs.text:
A fiber is a path from a root (or soma) to a leaf node.

# All fibers
fibers = forest.get_fibers()

# Only fibers that originate at a soma node (type=1)
fibers = forest.get_fibers(only_from_soma=True)

# Filter by minimum length
fibers = forest.get_fibers(min_length=20.0)

# Fibers that pass through a region of interest
fibers = forest.get_fibers_by_roi(roi)

# Single fiber to a specific leaf node
fiber = forest.get_fiber_by_leaf(leaves[0])

docs.title: LCA (Lowest Common Ancestor)
docs.text:
For distance calculations between arbitrary nodes:

forest.get_lca_preprocess(node_num=forest.size())
ancestor_id = forest.get_lca(node_u, node_v)

docs.title: Saving
docs.text: forest.save_to_file("output.swc")

docs.title: SwcNode — Individual node
docs.text:
Every node in SwcForest is a SwcNode instance:

node = forest.get_node_list()[0]

print(node.nid)            # node ID
print(node.ntype)          # 1=soma, 2=axon, 3=basal, 4=apical, 5=other
print(node.coord)          # EuclideanPoint3D
print(node.radius)
print(node.root_length)    # accumulated path length from root

# Distance to another node
d = node.distance(other_node, mode="3D")   # or "2D" for XY only

# Topology checks
node.is_root()
node.is_leaf()

# Sub-tree operations
descendants = node.get_subtree_node_list()
subtree_len = node.get_subtree_length()
leaves = node.get_subtree_leafs()
fibers = node.get_subtree_fibers()

docs.title: SwcFiber — A single root-to-leaf path
docs.text:
fiber = fibers[0]

print(fiber.length)           # path length (float)
print(fiber.coords)           # ndarray (N, 3)
print(fiber.center)           # mean coordinate

# Nearest node on this fiber to an external point
nearest = fiber.get_nearest_node([x, y, z], return_dist=False)

# Compare two fibers
iou    = fiber.cal_iou(other_fiber, dist_threshold=3.0)
is_sub = fiber.is_sub_fiber_of(other_fiber)
overlap = fiber.get_overlap_length_with(other_fiber, dist_threshold=3.0)

docs.title: SWC ↔ Mask Conversion
docs.text: swclib supports bidirectional conversion between SWC neuron structures and 3D binary mask images (volumetric arrays stored as TIFF files).

docs.title: SWC → 3D Mask (Swc2Mask)
docs.text: Swc2Mask renders an SWC neuron into a 3D binary (or distance-transformed) mask volume.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text:
from swclib.image.swc2mask import Swc2Mask

converter = Swc2Mask(
    shape=(300, 300, 300),  # output volume shape (Z, Y, X)
    scale=(1, 1, 1),        # voxel size (sx, sy, sz)
    radius=1,               # neurite radius in voxels
    method="line",          # rendering method: "line" or "sphere_cone"
)

mask = converter.run("neuron.swc", out_file="neuron_mask.tif")
# mask: numpy array of shape (Z, Y, X), dtype uint8

docs.title: Rendering methods
docs.text: Method Description "line" Fast distance-transform–based rendering. Suitable for thin structures. "sphere_cone" Renders each node as a sphere and each edge as a truncated cone. More accurate for thick structures.

docs.title: Anisotropic voxels
docs.text:
If your image has different voxel sizes in each axis (common in confocal microscopy), pass the voxel size via scale:

# XY resolution: 0.5 µm/vox, Z resolution: 1.0 µm/vox
converter = Swc2Mask(
    shape=(200, 512, 512),
    scale=(0.5, 0.5, 1.0),
    radius=1,
)

docs.title: Low-level functions
docs.text:
For fine-grained control you can call the rendering functions directly:

from swclib.image.swc2mask import swc_to_mask_line, swc_to_mask_sphere_cone
from swclib.data.swc import Swc

swc = Swc("neuron.swc")

# Line method
mask = swc_to_mask_line(swc, shape=(300,300,300), scale=(1,1,1), max_radius=3)

# Sphere+cone method
mask = swc_to_mask_sphere_cone(swc, shape=(300,300,300), foreground_value=1, r_scale=1.0)

docs.title: 3D Mask → SWC (Mask2Swc)
docs.text: Mask2Swc converts a binary 3D mask to an SWC reconstruction through a pipeline of skeletonization, graph building, and post-processing.

docs.title: Pipeline overview
docs.text:
Binary mask
    │
    ▼  skeletonize (scikit-image)
Skeleton
    │
    ▼  build_graph_from_skeleton
NetworkX Graph
    │
    ├─ remove_isolated_nodes
    ├─ merge_fibers           (merge nearby parallel branches)
    ├─ remove_short_fibers    (prune noise)
    ├─ remove_short_branchs   (prune short side branches)
    └─ refine_fibers          (smooth + resample)
    │
    ▼
SWC File

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text:
from swclib.image.mask2swc import Mask2Swc
import tifffile

mask = tifffile.imread("neuron_mask.tif")  # binary uint8 array

converter = Mask2Swc(
    voxel_size=(1.0, 1.0, 1.0),    # physical size of each voxel (µm)
    thres_fiber_min_len=20.0,       # remove isolated fibers shorter than this
    thres_branch_min_len=15.0,      # prune side branches shorter than this
    node_sample_distance=2.0,       # output node spacing
)

converter.run(mask, "output.swc", soma_path="soma.txt", radius=0.1)

docs.title: With soma annotation
docs.text:
If you have a soma annotation file, pass it via soma_path to improve root assignment:

converter.run(mask, "output.swc", soma_path="soma_annotation.txt")

The soma file format matches the output of SwcSoma.save_somas_to_file.

docs.title: Configuration parameters
docs.text: Parameter Default Description voxel_size (1,1,1) Physical voxel dimensions (sx, sy, sz) in µm connectivity 26 Neighbor connectivity for skeletonization (6 or 26) thres_fiber_min_len 20.0 Minimum length to keep an isolated fiber (µm) thres_branch_min_len 15.0 Minimum length to keep a side branch (µm) thres_segment_merge_angle — Max angle (°) between fibers to be merged thres_segment_merge_dist — Max endpoint distance for fiber merging (µm) smooth_window_size — Savitzky-Golay smoothing window size smooth_poly — Savitzky-Golay polynomial order node_sample_distance 2.0 Output node spacing after resampling

docs.title: Verbose mode
docs.text:
converter.run(mask, "output.swc", verbose=True)
# Prints step-by-step statistics for each pipeline stage

docs.title: Soma mask generation
docs.text:
SwcSoma provides utilities to render detected somas to a volumetric mask:

from swclib.data.swc_soma import read_soma_from_file, create_soma_mask

somas = read_soma_from_file("soma_annotation.txt")
soma_mask = create_soma_mask(somas, volume_shape=(300, 300, 300), out_path="soma_mask.tif")

docs.title: Reconstruction Metrics
docs.text:
swclib provides four complementary metrics for comparing a predicted neuron reconstruction against a gold standard. All metrics share the same interface:

result = metric.run(gold_swc_path, pred_swc_path)

gold_swc_path and pred_swc_path are paths to SWC files. The return value is always a plain dict.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Metric Class What it measures SSD SSDMetric Mean spatial distance between node sets Length LengthMetric Edge-level path coverage (precision/recall) Keypoint KeypointMetric Detection of branch and leaf points Fiber FiberMetric Path-level (root-to-leaf) matching

docs.title: SSD Metric
docs.text: The Spatial Distance (SSD) metric resamples both trees to uniform node spacing, then computes the mean distance between each node and its nearest neighbor in the other tree.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from swclib.metrics.ssd_metric import SSDMetric

metric = SSDMetric(
    min_distance=2.0,       # resampling step (µm)
    scale=(1, 1, 1),        # coordinate scaling
)
result = metric.run("gold.swc", "pred.swc")

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Key Description sd Mean bidirectional spatial distance sd_gt2pred Mean distance from gold nodes to nearest predicted node sd_pred2gt Mean distance from predicted nodes to nearest gold node ssd Mean of distances exceeding min_distance ssd_gt2pred SSD in gold→pred direction ssd_pred2gt SSD in pred→gold direction ssd_percent Fraction of nodes with distance > min_distance

docs.title: Length Metric
docs.text: The Length metric compares reconstructions at the edge level. Each edge (segment between two consecutive nodes) in the gold standard is matched against edges in the predicted tree within a spatial radius.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from swclib.metrics.length_metric import LengthMetric

metric = LengthMetric(
    radius_threshold=5.0,   # matching radius (µm)
    length_threshold=2.0,   # minimum edge length to evaluate (µm)
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
    resample_step=1.0,      # resampling step for edge comparison
)
result = metric.run("gold.swc", "pred.swc")

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Key Description precision Fraction of predicted length that matches gold recall Fraction of gold length that is covered by prediction f1_score Harmonic mean of precision and recall TP Matched edge length FP Unmatched predicted edge length FN Unmatched gold edge length num_gt Total gold edge count num_pred Total predicted edge count

docs.title: Keypoint Metric
docs.text: The Keypoint metric evaluates detection of topologically significant points: branch nodes (degree ≥ 3) and leaf nodes (degree 1).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from swclib.metrics.keypoint_metric import KeypointMetric

metric = KeypointMetric(
    keypoint_types=["branch", "leaf"],
    threshold_dis=5.0,      # matching distance threshold (µm)
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
    use_category=True,      # report branch and leaf stats separately
)
result = metric.run("gold.swc", "pred.swc")

docs.title: Output (combined mode, use_category=False)
docs.text: Key Description precision Keypoint detection precision recall Keypoint detection recall f1 F1 score TP / FP / FN Matched / extra / missed keypoints

docs.title: Output (per-category mode, use_category=True)
docs.text:
The dict contains nested sub-dicts for each keypoint type:

result["branch"]["precision"]
result["branch"]["recall"]
result["branch"]["f1"]

result["leaf"]["precision"]
result["leaf"]["recall"]
result["leaf"]["f1"]

docs.title: Fiber Metric
docs.text:
The Fiber metric is the most comprehensive metric. It extracts all root-to-leaf paths (fibers) from both trees and matches them using Intersection-over-Union (IoU).

A fiber is counted as a True Positive (TP) if its IoU with a matched gold fiber exceeds iou_threshold.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from swclib.metrics.fiber_metric import FiberMetric

metric = FiberMetric(
    iou_threshold=0.8,          # minimum IoU for a match to count as TP
    dist_threshold=5.0,         # spatial tolerance (µm) for IoU calculation
    dist_sample=1.0,            # resampling step for IoU calculation
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
    only_from_soma=True,        # only evaluate fibers starting at type=1 nodes
    min_fiber_length=20.0,      # ignore fibers shorter than this (µm)
    align_roots=True,           # align tree roots before comparison
    align_roots_threshold=20.0, # max distance to match roots
    with_direction=False,       # consider fiber direction
    use_category=True,          # separate axon (type=2) and dendrite (type=3/4) stats
)
result = metric.run("gold.swc", "pred.swc")

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Key Description precision Fraction of predicted fibers that match a gold fiber recall Fraction of gold fibers that are matched by a prediction f1_score Harmonic mean TP / FP / FN Fiber-level counts num_gt Number of gold fibers num_pred Number of predicted fibers iou_matched Mean IoU among matched fiber pairs iou_all Mean IoU across all gold fibers (unmatched = 0) ious Per-fiber IoU values matches List of matched (gold_fiber, pred_fiber) pairs FN_fiber_ids Node IDs of unmatched gold fibers

With use_category=True, per-category stats appear under result["axon"] and result["dendrite"].

With return_fibers=True, the actual SwcFiber objects are returned under result["gold_fibers"] and result["pred_fibers"].

docs.title: Example: inspect unmatched fibers
docs.text:
result = metric.run("gold.swc", "pred.swc", return_fibers=True)

for i, fiber in enumerate(result["gold_fibers"]):
    iou = result["ious"][i]
    if iou < metric.iou_threshold:
        print(f"Unmatched gold fiber, length={fiber.length:.1f}, IoU={iou:.2f}")

docs.title: Batch evaluation with MetricManager
docs.text:
MetricManager runs multiple metrics on a dataset of reconstruction pairs and aggregates results.

from swclib.metrics.manager import MetricManager

manager = MetricManager(
    metric_names=["ssd", "length", "keypoints", "fiber"],
    collect_method="micro",  # aggregate TP/FP/FN across all pairs ("micro")
    scale=(1, 1, 1),
)

# Optionally override per-metric parameters
manager.update_metric_params({
    "fiber": {"iou_threshold": 0.8, "dist_threshold": 5.0},
    "keypoints": {"threshold_dis": 5.0},
})

# Add reconstruction pairs
for gold_path, pred_path in zip(gold_files, pred_files):
    manager.add_data(gold_path, pred_path)

# Aggregate and save
summary = manager.collect(save_path="results.json")
print(summary)

docs.title: Collect methods
docs.text:
Method Description "micro" Sum all TP/FP/FN across the dataset, then compute final P/R/F1 "macro" Compute P/R/F1 per pair, then average

Micro averaging is typically preferred for imbalanced datasets (neurons with very different fiber counts).

docs.title: Choosing the right metric
docs.text: Scenario Recommended metric Quick sanity check SSD Evaluate path tracing completeness Length Evaluate topology accuracy Keypoint Comprehensive morphology evaluation Fiber Full benchmark pipeline MetricManager with all four

docs.title: Whole-Brain Image I/O
docs.text: Whole-brain imaging datasets are typically stored as thousands of 2D TIFF slices rather than a single 3D volume. Loading the entire stack into memory is impractical. WBTReader addresses this by reading only the requested sub-region in parallel.

docs.title: WBTReader
docs.text: WBTReader reads a directory of 2D TIFF slices and exposes a 3D volume interface. Reads are parallelized with a thread or process pool.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
from swclib.whole_brain.tifreader import WBTReader

reader = WBTReader(
    slice_dir="/data/brain/xy_slices/",
    slice_name_pattern=r"slice_(\d+)\.tif",   # regex with one capture group = Z index
)

slice_name_pattern must contain exactly one capture group matching the Z index of each slice file.

docs.title: Inspect volume dimensions
docs.text:
depth, height, width = reader.get_dimensions()
print(f"Volume: {depth} x {height} x {width}")

(x1, y1, z1), (x2, y2, z2) = reader.get_bbox()

docs.title: Read a 3D sub-region
docs.text:
volume = reader.read_region(
    start=(x1, y1, z1),        # inclusive start corner (x, y, z)
    end=(x2, y2, z2),          # exclusive end corner (x, y, z)
    mode="tiff",               # backend: "tiff" or "raster"
    num_workers=16,            # parallel worker count
    parallel_backend="thread", # "thread" or "process"
    padding=None,              # optional (px, py, pz) padding
)
# returns numpy array of shape (z2-z1, y2-y1, x2-x1)

docs.title: Read modes
docs.text: Mode Library Best for "tiff" tifffile Standard TIFF files "raster" rasterio GeoTIFF or large tiled TIFF with spatial metadata

docs.title: Parallel backends
docs.text: Backend Use when "thread" I/O-bound reads (most common case) "process" CPU-bound decoding (e.g., heavy compression)

docs.title: Typical workflow
docs.text:
from swclib.whole_brain.tifreader import WBTReader
from swclib.image.mask2swc import Mask2Swc

reader = WBTReader(
    slice_dir="/data/brain/slices/",
    slice_name_pattern=r"img_z(\d{4})\.tif",
)

# Read a local region around a neuron of interest
block = reader.read_region(
    start=(2000, 3000, 100),
    end=(2512, 3512, 200),
    num_workers=32,
)

# Reconstruct SWC from local block
converter = Mask2Swc(voxel_size=(0.5, 0.5, 1.0))
converter.run(block, "local_neuron.swc")

docs.title: SwcReader — SWC file loading for whole-brain coordinates
docs.text:
For whole-brain SWC files with large coordinate values, use SwcReader from the same module:

from swclib.whole_brain.swc_reader import SwcReader

nodes = SwcReader.read("whole_brain_neuron.swc")
# returns numpy array of shape (N, 7): [id, type, x, y, z, radius, parent]
